title: Frozens 1st Week in Japan Tops Evas All Time records
display_title: Frozens 1st Week in Japan Tops Evas All Time records
wikitext:
[]: Frozen's 1st Week in Japan Tops Eva's All-Time records             Quote Post by WunderBah » Tue Jul 22, 2014 11:36 pm http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2014-07-23/frozen-home-video-tops-evangelion-spirited-away-all-time-records-with-1.5-million-in-japan/.76945 Thought it deserved its own topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Jul 22, 2014 11:43 pm I. Friggin. Hate. FROZEN. It robbed "the Wind Rises" of the best animated Film Award it rightfully deserved! Now this? Oh. . .now I'm mad.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Tue Jul 22, 2014 11:59 pm Is Frozen that bad?

[]:             Quote Post by Adaminator1 » Wed Jul 23, 2014 12:07 am WunderBah wrote: Is Frozen that bad?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jul 23, 2014 12:19 am Lots of people like it I don't care for it.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Jul 23, 2014 12:27 am ^ I take it you're not into Disney that much. Now, I both Agree and Disagree with you. I really like Frozen, cause it is a good movie, but I agree with the over-hypeness it's getting. But, hey, if people prefer an optimistic tale of learning to accept yourself over a harsh tale of learning to accept yourself, then hey, that's their choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jul 23, 2014 12:56 am Oh I love Disney. . .But it's taken a major nosedive in terms of quality lately. Dreamworks is putting out the better product these days. Not to mention noone outside of Asia is even bothering to make high quality hand drawn animation anymore. Evangelion falls to Frozen because frozen has the family audience supplementing the otaku fanbase. It appeals to everyone Eva is way to harsh and dark for kids. Especially 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jul 23, 2014 2:44 am Ray wrote: Not to mention noone outside of Asia is even bothering to make high quality hand drawn animation anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Jul 23, 2014 2:48 am Already a big fan, but Legend of Korra is produced and directed by Americans, but its animated in Korea. and about 3.0. . .agreed. That movie was just. . .blood and angst. DAMMIT ANNO!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jul 23, 2014 2:59 am Ray wrote: and about 3.0. . .agreed. That movie was just. . .blood and angst.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Jul 23, 2014 10:01 am Considering Frozen is more popular than Christ in the US alone, this is hardly surprising. I get guests at work asking me who my favorite Frozen character is despite the fact that I have yet to see the movie (and I own it on BD and it's still in the wrapper). Even if it came neck and neck with Eva, I'm pretty sure it still would have done better than Q considering how Q's sales were lower than 2.22's. Everyone who knew NGE and entered Rebuild knew what to expect, that sooner or later the depressive stuff that is what made the series so famous would come back after the light-hearted shenanigans, the change from light-hearted to darker feels (and is) more sudden due to the film format.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Wed Jul 23, 2014 11:36 am Well luckily we still have another movie that could potentially gives us answers on how and why things came to be in Q before writing the whole thing off. If Anno could info dump the 13 angel revelation from EoE with Misato in under a small amount of minutes without pacing suffering then i cannot see how he can't do the same for the time skip. And yes, i do understand how the film giving out little answers itself on key elements could lead to a frustrating experience for plenty of fans.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 23, 2014 12:07 pm WHY DOES SHE HAVE ICE MAGIC? WHY DID THEY NEVER EXPLAIN THAT SHIT?

[]: Re: Frozen's 1st Week in Japan Tops Eva's All-Time records             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jul 23, 2014 12:16 pm WunderBah wrote: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2014-07-23/frozen-home-video-tops-evangelion-spirited-away-all-time-records-with-1.5-million-in-japan/.76945

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jul 23, 2014 3:09 pm ^ I guess it's appealing to a wider audience than Eva is. Just because something sells more does not necessarily mean that it's the better option, though. (Even though Frozen is pretty damn good.)

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jul 23, 2014 3:18 pm To everyone I just say.... LET IT GO! LET IT GOOOOOOOOO!!

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Jul 23, 2014 4:18 pm http://imgur.com/gallery/EaW61

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Jul 23, 2014 4:24 pm Though, honestly, between the two sources should we really be surprised? When it comes to the content, Optimism isn't Naivety, and Cynicism isn't Wisdom.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed Jul 23, 2014 4:43 pm I'm always surprised when Japan loves American stuff more than the stuff they create.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Jul 23, 2014 4:54 pm ^ It's the same with Americans like Japanese stuff more than our product. It's the exotic culture angle that entices our curiosity. That....and the massive amounts of porn.
